title: Secrets in logs are exposure
description: A secret printed to logs, errors, traces, crash dumps, prompts, or artifacts should be treated as exposed even if the primary code path is fixed later.
body: Use when code handles tokens, credentials, signing keys, connection strings, session IDs, reset links, or API responses containing credentials. Do not use to display or copy the secret value. Actions: redact before emission, check derived artifacts, and mark rotation or incident follow-up for humans. Validation signals include sanitized outputs and absence from generated files. Risk boundary: real secret discovery must trigger human incident handling.
